title: Folding Desktop Chalkboard Easel
summary: A pocket-hole-framed, hinged pair of chalkboard panels that fold flat for storage and pop open into a tabletop easel.
description:
Two identical picture-frame-style boxes, hinged together at the top and tied with a rope at the bottom to set how far they open — that's the entire mechanism. The frames are the only part that needs real precision; everything after that (painting, hinging, adding a carry handle) is forgiving.

Paint the chalkboard-paint hardboard panels before you fasten them into the frames, not after. It's far easier to get an even coat with the panel lying flat and unobstructed than to cut in around an assembled frame. If you're skipping a pocket hole jig, glue the frames and let them dry fully, then let the plywood backer panel do double duty — screw or nail through it into the frame joint for extra strength once the glue has set.

tools.note: Recommended for the edge-to-edge frame joints; glue-and-clamp works as a fallback.

materials.name: Plywood or hardboard, 1/4" or 1/8"
materials.note: Quarter sheet — the two chalkboard panel backers.

materials.name: Rope or wire
materials.note: Ties the two frames at the bottom so the easel stops at the right open angle.

steps.title: Build the two frames
steps.body: Build two identical picture-frame-style boxes. A pocket hole jig is the easiest way to join edge-to-edge like this; without one, glue the frames and let them dry fully, then rely on the plywood backer panel (added in a later step) to reinforce the joints with screws or nails once the glue has set.

steps.title: Attach the chalk ledge
steps.body: Attach the ledge to the bottom of one frame with a countersunk screw driven from the back — that gives the strongest joint for a ledge that'll get some daily bumping.

steps.title: Paint and fit the chalkboard panels
steps.body: Paint the hardboard panels with chalkboard paint before fitting them to the backs of the frames — it's much easier to get even coverage on a flat, unobstructed panel than to paint around an assembled frame. It also helps to paint the frames themselves at this stage, before the chalkboard panels are attached.

steps.title: Hinge, tie, and add a handle
steps.body: Hinge the two frames together at the top. Tie a rope between the bottoms of the frames to set how far the easel opens and hold it stable, then add a handle to the top for carrying.

images.alt: Folding desktop easel with two hinged chalkboard panels
